# Local Ads Optimization

Use this skill after the offer, proof, and content angles are already defined.

This skill assumes a local-service funnel:
content or ad -> inbox or form -> qualification -> survey -> quote -> close

## When To Use

- The user needs a paid campaign plan.
- The user wants to choose between message, lead, or search intent campaigns.
- The user wants KPI targets and optimization logic.
- The user wants a local retargeting plan.

## Procedure

1. Confirm the target action:
   - inbox
   - lead form
   - phone call
   - search click
2. Choose one main campaign at a time, not all channels at once.
3. Match the channel to intent:
   - Facebook: social proof plus inbox conversion
   - TikTok: hook-led video plus remarketing
   - Google: high-intent search terms if volume exists
4. Build the first campaign brief using [campaign brief template](./assets/campaign-brief-template.md).
5. Test only a small number of variables in each round:
   - angle
   - hook
   - area
   - CTA
6. Review KPIs using [local ads metrics](./references/local-ads-metrics.md).
7. Keep winners, pause weak creatives, and feed learnings back into content.

## Rules

- Do not scale before checking lead quality.
- Local proof usually beats broad branding for home-improvement services.
- Use area names and project-type relevance in copy when appropriate.
- Separate premium audiences from price-sensitive mass audiences when the offer differs.

## References

- [campaign brief template](./assets/campaign-brief-template.md)
- [local ads metrics](./references/local-ads-metrics.md)
